verses.text: These are the words of the covenant which the LORD commanded Moses to make with Israel in Moab, beside the covenant at Horeb. Moses said: Ye have seen all that the LORD did in Egypt, the great temptations, the signs, those great miracles: yet the LORD hath not given you an heart to perceive, eyes to see, ears to hear, unto this day. Guidance: A covenant beside the first — God renews what the wilderness generation ratified at Sinai. And the painful diagnosis: you saw, but you did not really see. Faith requires the gift of a soft heart, and God has not given it yet. Paul will quote this verse in Romans 11. Sight is given; understanding is a separate grace.

I have led you forty years in the wilderness: your clothes are not waxen old upon you, thy shoe is not waxen old upon thy foot. Ye have not eaten bread, neither drunk wine or strong drink: that ye might know that I am the LORD your God. Sihon king of Heshbon, Og king of Bashan, came out against us; we smote them, gave their land to the Reubenites, Gadites, half tribe of Manassites. Keep therefore the words of this covenant, do them, that ye may prosper. Guidance: Forty years of unworn shoes, unaging clothes, the manna instead of bread — God names His logistic miracle. He fed you in the desert and gave you two kingdoms before the river. Now keep the covenant. Memory of grace is the foundation of obedience.

Ye stand this day all of you before the LORD your God: captains of tribes, elders, officers, men of Israel, little ones, wives, the stranger in thy camp, from the hewer of thy wood unto the drawer of thy water: that thou shouldest enter into covenant with the LORD thy God this day: that He may establish thee today for a people unto Himself, that He may be unto thee a God, as He sware unto thy fathers. Guidance: Everyone is named. Generals down to firewood-collectors, men and women, citizens and immigrants. No one stood outside the covenant by status. The poorest stranger fetching water that morning was a covenant party with God. Belonging is by oath, not by class.

Neither with you only do I make this covenant and this oath; but with him that standeth here with us this day before the LORD our God, and also with him that is not here with us this day. Guidance: The covenant reaches forward — every Israelite not yet born is signed in. Every reader of this verse is included by intention. The terms of Moab were drafted for descendants the writer would never meet. God's covenants are made to outlive the parties present.

Ye know how we dwelt in Egypt; ye have seen their abominations, idols of wood, stone, silver, gold. Lest there be among you a root that beareth gall and wormwood; if when he heareth this curse, he bless himself in his heart: I shall have peace, though I walk in the imagination of mine heart — the LORD will not spare him; His anger shall smoke against him; the LORD shall blot out his name. Guidance: The hidden apostate. He hears the curses and tells himself: not me, I'll be fine. God names this self-soothing for what it is — a smoking offense. To bless yourself while disobeying is the most dangerous prayer.

The generation to come of your children, the stranger from a far land, when they see the plagues of that land — brimstone, salt, burning, nothing groweth, like Sodom and Gomorrah — all nations shall say: Wherefore hath the LORD done thus? Men shall answer: Because they forsook the covenant of the LORD; they went and served other gods. The LORD rooted them out of their land in anger, cast them into another land, as it is this day. Guidance: God's discipline is not hidden; it becomes a tourist's question. Foreigners walking past Israel's wasteland would point and ask, and the answer would have to come from inside the people: we broke faith. Even discipline preaches when it endures.

The secret things belong unto the LORD our God: but those things which are revealed belong unto us and to our children for ever, that we may do all the words of this law. Guidance: One verse that draws the line. What He has not shown is His; what He has shown is ours. Stop trying to pry into His silences; start obeying what He has spoken. The mystery is the LORD's; the law is the family's. Most spiritual anxiety dissolves under this verse.
